data.acceptance:
1. Foolery settings include one persisted interactive-session inactivity timeout with a default of 10 minutes, and the Settings UI exposes it on a user-editable control.
2. Interactive watchdog enforcement is configured from one shared settings/runtime source above the harness layer rather than from per-agent `watchdogTimeoutMs` literals.
3. The current interactive Codex, Copilot, OpenCode, and Gemini paths all consume that shared timeout behavior with no harness-specific timeout policy remaining.
4. The shared design makes a newly added interactive harness inherit the inactivity timeout automatically when it uses the standard interactive session abstraction.
5. Tests cover the default value, settings persistence/normalization, and shared interactive watchdog behavior.
